response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer, but the role's hard requirements are largely unmet: no indication of Japanese proficiency (the resume lists English, Romanian, and German), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, and only brief C/C++ exposure (a ~6-month stint at National Instruments) versus the required strong C-for-UNIX proficiency. There is no evidence of L2/L3 networking, switch products, MACsec, 802.1X, or embedded security work, making the profile a poor fit despite strong general seniority.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Systems-level and performance-oriented work (low-latency DLang services, async I/O, Linux-based audio thesis project)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — a hard requirement for this role
Minimal professional C experience and no Linux kernel source analysis or modification background
No networking/security domain experience (L2/L3, switch products, MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF)

key_factors.factor: Lack of C/UNIX and Linux kernel expertise
